task_statement: Design secure access to AWS resources
knowledge_required: Access controls and management across multiple accounts; AWS federated access and identity services (IAM Identity Center, Cognito); AWS global infrastructure (Regions, AZs); AWS security best practices (least privilege, root user protection); AWS Shared Responsibility Model
skills_required: Apply AWS security best practices to IAM users and root users; design a flexible authorization model with users, groups, roles, and policies; design a security strategy for multiple AWS accounts; determine the appropriate use of resource-based policies, service control policies, and trust policies; determine when to federate a directory service with IAM roles
notes: All 8 KG concepts mapped; high coverage

knowledge_required: Application configuration and credentials security; AWS service endpoints; control ports, protocols, and network traffic on AWS; secure application access; security services with appropriate use cases (Cognito, GuardDuty, Macie, Inspector); threat vectors external to AWS (DDoS, SQLi, etc.)
skills_required: Design VPC architectures with security components (security groups, route tables, NACLs, NAT gateways); determine network segmentation strategies (public and private subnets); integrate AWS services to secure applications (AWS Shield, WAF, Cognito); secure external network connections to and from the AWS Cloud
notes: Missing dedicated waf-rules concept; create or rely on shield-waf

knowledge_required: Data access and governance; data recovery; data retention and classification; encryption and appropriate key management
skills_required: Align AWS technologies to meet compliance requirements; encrypt data at rest (KMS); encrypt data in transit (ACM, TLS); implement access policies for encryption keys; implement data backups and replication; implement policies for data access, lifecycle, and protection; rotate encryption keys and renew certificates
notes: Strong coverage; KMS rotation could deserve its own concept later

task_statement: Design scalable and loosely coupled architectures
knowledge_required: AWS managed services with appropriate use cases (SQS, SNS, EventBridge, Step Functions); caching strategies; design principles for microservices (stateless workloads vs stateful); event-driven architectures; horizontal scaling vs vertical scaling; how to appropriately use edge accelerators (CloudFront); how to migrate applications into containers; load balancing concepts (ELB types); multi-tier architectures; queuing and messaging concepts; serverless technologies and patterns; storage types with associated characteristics; the orchestration of containers (ECS, EKS); when to use read replicas
skills_required: Design event-driven, microservice, and/or multi-tier architectures based on requirements; determine scaling strategies; determine the AWS services required to achieve loose coupling; determine when to use containers; determine when to use serverless; recommend appropriate compute, storage, networking, and database services for scalability

task_statement: Design highly available and/or fault-tolerant architectures
knowledge_required: AWS global infrastructure (multi-Region, multi-AZ); DR strategies (backup-and-restore, pilot light, warm standby, multi-site active-active); how AWS services achieve fault tolerance; immutable infrastructure; load balancing; networking concepts (Route 53 routing policies); replication; storage options and characteristics (durability and availability); workload visibility (X-Ray)
skills_required: Determine automation strategies to ensure infrastructure integrity; determine the AWS services required to provide a highly available and/or fault-tolerant architecture; identify metrics based on business requirements to deliver a highly available solution; implement designs to mitigate single points of failure; implement strategies to ensure data durability

task_statement: Determine high-performing and/or scalable storage solutions
knowledge_required: Hybrid storage solutions to meet business requirements; storage services with appropriate use cases (S3, EBS, EFS, FSx, Storage Gateway); storage types with associated characteristics (object, block, file)
skills_required: Determine storage services and configurations that meet performance demands; determine storage services that can scale to accommodate future needs

task_statement: Design high-performing and elastic compute solutions
knowledge_required: AWS compute services with appropriate use cases (EC2, Lambda, ECS, EKS, Fargate, Batch); distributed computing concepts; queuing and messaging concepts (publish-subscribe); scalability capabilities with appropriate use cases (Auto Scaling, Spot Fleet); serverless technologies and patterns (Lambda, Fargate); the orchestration of containers (ECS, EKS); when to use edge accelerators (CloudFront, Global Accelerator)
skills_required: Decouple workloads to enable components to scale independently; identify metrics and conditions to perform scaling actions; select appropriate compute options and features (EC2 instance types) to meet business requirements; select appropriate resource type and size (Lambda memory) to meet business requirements

knowledge_required: AWS global infrastructure (replication, latency); caching strategies and services (ElastiCache, DAX); data access patterns (read-heavy vs write-heavy); database capacity planning (storage capacity, throughput); database connections and proxies (RDS Proxy); database engines with appropriate use cases (relational vs NoSQL); database replication (read replicas); database types and services (Aurora, DynamoDB, Redshift, Neptune)
skills_required: Apply database concepts, design principles, and architecture patterns; determine an appropriate database engine; determine an appropriate database type; integrate caching to meet business requirements

task_statement: Determine high-performing and/or scalable network architectures
knowledge_required: Edge networking services with appropriate use cases (CloudFront, Global Accelerator); how to design network architecture (subnet tiers, routing); load balancing concepts (ALB, NLB, GWLB); network connection options (VPN, Direct Connect)
skills_required: Create a network topology for various architectures (public vs private subnets); determine network configurations that can scale to accommodate future needs; determine the appropriate placement of resources to meet business requirements; select the appropriate load balancing strategy

task_statement: Determine high-performing data ingestion and transformation solutions
knowledge_required: Data analytics and visualization services with appropriate use cases (Athena, Lake Formation, QuickSight, Redshift); data ingestion patterns; data transfer services with appropriate use cases (DataSync, Storage Gateway, Snow Family); data transformation services (Glue, EMR, Lambda); secure access to ingestion access points; sizes and speeds needed to meet business requirements; streaming data services with appropriate use cases (Kinesis Data Streams, Kinesis Data Firehose, Managed Service for Apache Flink)
skills_required: Build and secure data lakes; design data streaming architectures; design data transfer solutions; implement visualization strategies; select appropriate compute options for data processing (EMR); select appropriate configurations for ingestion
notes: Full data-lake stack now in KG (Athena/Glue/Lake Formation/EMR/QuickSight)

knowledge_required: Access options for data (Athena vs Glacier retrieval); AWS cost management tools and services (Cost Explorer, Budgets, Cost and Usage Report); AWS storage services with appropriate use cases; backup strategies; block storage options (EBS volume types and HDD vs SSD); data lifecycles; hybrid storage options (DataSync, Storage Gateway); storage access patterns; storage tiering (S3 Standard, IA, Glacier); storage types with associated characteristics
skills_required: Design appropriate storage strategies (S3 Intelligent-Tiering); determine the correct storage size for a workload; determine the lowest-cost method of transferring data for a workload (Snow vs DataSync); manage S3 object lifecycles; select appropriate backup and/or archive solutions; select appropriate data access and retrieval patterns; select an appropriate storage tier

knowledge_required: AWS cost management service features (instance usage reports); AWS cost management tools (Cost Explorer, Budgets); AWS purchasing options (Reserved, Savings Plans, Spot); distributed compute strategies (edge processing); hybrid compute options (Outposts, Snow); instance types, families, and sizes (memory optimized, compute optimized); optimization of compute utilization (containers, serverless)
skills_required: Determine an appropriate load balancing strategy; determine appropriate scaling methods and strategies for elastic workloads; determine cost-effective AWS compute services with appropriate use cases (Spot for batch); determine the required availability for different classes of workloads; select the appropriate instance family; select the appropriate instance size

knowledge_required: AWS cost management service features; AWS cost management tools (Cost Explorer); caching strategies (offload reads from DB); data retention policies; database capacity planning; database connections and proxies; database engines with appropriate use cases; database replication; database types and services
skills_required: Apply the appropriate database type and size; determine appropriate auto-scaling strategies (Aurora Serverless, DynamoDB On-Demand); determine the appropriate data store; migrate database schemas and data to different locations and/or different database engines (DMS); plan for backup and retention

knowledge_required: AWS cost management tools; load balancing concepts; NAT gateways (NAT instance vs NAT gateway costs); network connectivity options (VPN vs Direct Connect costs); network routing, topology, and peering; network services with appropriate use cases
skills_required: Configure appropriate NAT gateway types; configure appropriate network connections (VPN vs Direct Connect); configure appropriate routes (private vs public traffic); determine strategic networking decisions to reduce cost; recommend appropriate network features based on cost (VPC endpoints to reduce NAT cost); select an appropriate bandwidth allocation
